prompt:
My contacts are split across two half-dead address books and every 'search' tool I try is uselessly ASCII-brained. I want a small contact book CLI, entry point `contacts.py`, book in a JSON file via `--book FILE`.

`add NAME [--email E] [--phone P]` stores a contact, silent on success. `search QUERY` prints matches one per line as the name, then ` <email>` if present, then ` <space>phone` if present — e.g. `José García <Jose@Garcia.ES> +34-600-111-222` — sorted by normalized name (ties by normalized email, missing email first). Matching is the whole point: substring against name OR email, but case-insensitive and diacritic-insensitive done properly — searching `jose garcia` or `GARCÍA` finds José García, and searching `gross` finds Jürgen Groß (real Unicode case folding, not .lower()). Stored capitalization/accents are never altered — display always shows what I typed in. No matches: print nothing, exit 1, grep-style.

`merge OTHER.json` imports another book file of the same format. Two entries are the same person when normalized names match AND their emails match case-insensitively OR at least one of the two has no email; compare each incoming contact against existing ones in book order, first match wins. On a match, fill in whatever the existing entry is missing (email, phone) from the incoming one — existing values always win conflicts, names keep the existing spelling. Print `added N, merged M`. Merging the same file twice must be idempotent (second run: added 0). Missing file: exit 2.

`export FILE.vcf` writes vCard 3.0 for my phone: per contact (sorted like search results) the lines BEGIN:VCARD / VERSION:3.0 / FN:name / EMAIL:... (only if set) / TEL:... (only if set) / END:VCARD. The vCard spec demands CRLF line endings — every line, including the last, ends with \r\n — and the file must be UTF-8.

test_contacts.py is the acceptance contract (leave it as-is), run `python3 test_contacts.py`. Structure the implementation as several modules — I'd expect the Unicode normalization, book storage/merge, and vCard writer to be importable pieces, with contacts.py as the CLI.
